Class TransactionAdapter

java.lang.Object
javax.swing.table.AbstractTableModel
Views.TransactionAdapter
All Implemented Interfaces:
Serializable, TableModel
public class TransactionAdapter extends AbstractTableModel
A Swing TableModel adapter for displaying a list of Expense objects in a JTable.

Maps expense fields to five columns: ID, Amount, Category, Date, and Notes. Supports dynamic data updates via setExpenses(List).

See Also:

      public TransactionAdapter(List<Expense> expenses)

  • Method Details

    • setExpenses

      public void setExpenses(List<Expense> expenses)
      Updates the displayed expense list and notifies the table to refresh.
      Parameters:
      expenses - the new list of Expense objects to display

    • getRowCount

      public int getRowCount()
      Returns the number of rows in the table (one per expense).
      Returns:
      the number of expenses in the list

    • getColumnCount

      public int getColumnCount()
      Returns the number of columns in the table.
      Returns:
      the total number of columns (5)

    • getColumnName

      public String getColumnName(int col)
      Returns the display name of the specified column.
      Specified by:
      getColumnName in interface TableModel
      Overrides:
      getColumnName in class AbstractTableModel
      Parameters:
      col - the column index (0-based)
      Returns:
      the column header name

    • getValueAt

      public Object getValueAt(int rowIndex, int columnIndex)
      Returns the value to display in a specific table cell. Column mapping:
      • 0 – Expense ID
      • 1 – Amount (formatted with EGP)
      • 2 – Category ID
      • 3 – Timestamp (formatted as yyyy-MM-dd HH:mm)
      • 4 – Notes
      Parameters:
      rowIndex - the row index of the cell
      columnIndex - the column index of the cell
      Returns:
      the cell value, or null for unknown columns
